description: §65.3.2 / §65.9 — a program-scope `<theme>` structural element declaring named token VALUES (`brand`/`danger`/`line`) is RECOGNIZED (parses to a theme-decl node) and is a valid locus (program scope, §65.9). No parse / context / structural-placement error fires: `<theme>` is reclaimed as a scrml-defined structural element (§65.9), NOT a misplaced element or an unknown tag. Phase-A recognition proof.
rationale: §65.9: `<theme>` is a scrml-defined structural element (sibling of `<program>`/`<page>`), valid at program scope. §65.3.2: it declares named token values. A well-formed program-scope `<theme>` block fires none of E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED / E-CTX-001 / E-CTX-003 / E-SYNTAX-002, and no E-THEME-* code (the token block is valid). NOTE (Nominal): use-site token lowering (`color: brand` → `var(--brand)`) + E-THEME-TOKEN-UNKNOWN are Phase-B codegen (not yet emitted); this case asserts only the Phase-A recognition contract.
